# Standard Operating Procedure — DOC1025
**Title:** Cooling Bed Refractory / Lining Inspection SOP
**Procedure ID:** PROC-007  |  **Applies to:** EQ-CB-02 — Cooling Bed Line 2 (Cooling Bed)
**Location:** Mumbai Unit - Bay 1

## 1. Purpose
This procedure defines the standard method for the task titled "Cooling Bed Refractory / Lining Inspection SOP" on the cooling bed. It exists
to ensure the activity is carried out safely, consistently and in a way that protects personnel, equipment and product
quality, and to provide a training and audit reference for operators and maintenance staff.

## 2. Scope
This SOP applies to all operators, maintenance technicians and supervisors who operate or work on EQ-CB-02
(Cooling Bed Line 2) at Mumbai Unit - Bay 1. It covers the normal execution of the task and the
associated safety controls. It does not replace equipment-specific manufacturer instructions or statutory requirements.

## 3. Responsibilities
The shift supervisor authorises the activity and confirms isolation where required. The assigned technician executes the
steps and records completion. The equipment engineer maintains this procedure and reviews it after any related incident.

## 4. Prerequisites & PPE
Confirm the correct permit-to-work is in place, required isolations are applied, and standard PPE plus any
task-specific protection (as noted below) is worn. Ensure tools, spares and measuring equipment are available and
calibrated before starting.

## 5. Procedure Steps
1. Allow the cooling bed to cool to a safe inspection temperature and confirm atmosphere is safe to enter or approach.
2. Visually inspect refractory lining for cracking, spalling and wear, measuring residual thickness at marked reference points.
3. Photograph and log any zones below the minimum thickness threshold.
4. Raise a maintenance work order if wear exceeds the acceptance limit.

## 6. Safety Notes
- Hot bars up to ~950 degC - maintain exclusion zone along the bed.
- Moving walking beams and transfer mechanisms - lockout before entry.
- Sharp bar ends and skids - handling PPE required.
- Stop work and escalate to the shift supervisor if any parameter falls outside the normal operating band
  (reference: Bed length: as installed, Walking-beam pitch cycle automatic).

## 7. References
- Related equipment manual for EQ-CB-02.
- Applicable BIS / ASTM quality standards and The Factories Act, 1948 safety provisions.
- Plant CMMS maintenance schedule for the cooling bed.

## 8. Approval
Author: Naveen Malhotra (Equipment Engineer)
Approved by: Shalini Rao (Shift Supervisor, Shift A)
Revision: 1.0 — controlled document, review annually or after any related incident.
